content.claim: Hungary's birth cohorts are in steep, accelerating decline: 77,500 children born in 2024 (lowest on record) and approximately 72,000 in 2025 (7.1% fewer than 2024), with total fertility rate fallen to 1.31 children per woman, the lowest in 14 years and far below replacement level of 2.1.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Projections of future fertility remain uncertain and subject to behavioral and policy changes
Birth counts do not directly determine whether current school-track structures remain educationally or administratively viable
Year-to-year volatility in reporting or data revision possible
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
